title: Create a name string
prompt: Create a string variable called name with the text "Peter".
hint: Put the text inside quotation marks.

title: Create a message string
prompt: Create a string variable called message with the text "Hello, world!".
hint: Strings are text inside quotes.

title: Print a string variable
prompt: Create name = "Peter" and print it.
hint: Use print with the variable name.

title: Create a word string
prompt: Create a string variable called word with the text "apple".
hint: Use double quotes around apple.

title: Create a sentence string
prompt: Create a string variable called sentence with the text "I like Python.".
hint: Remember the period is part of the string.

title: Create an empty string
prompt: Create an empty string variable called empty.
hint: An empty string uses two quotation marks with nothing inside.

title: Use single quotes
prompt: Create a string variable a with the text hello using single quotes.
hint: Single quotes also work for strings.

title: Use double quotes
prompt: Create a string variable b with the text world using double quotes.
hint: Double quotes also work for strings.

title: Print single and double quote strings
prompt: Create a = 'hello' and b = "world", then print both.
hint: Use one print line for each variable.

title: Create a multi-line string
prompt: Create a variable called text that stores three lines: This is line 1. This is line 2. This is line 3.
answer:
text = """This is line 1.
This is line 2.
This is line 3."""
hint: Use triple quotes for a multi-line string.

title: Print a multi-line string
prompt: Create a multi-line string called text with three lines and print it.
starter:
text = """This is line 1.
This is line 2.
This is line 3."""
answer:
text = """This is line 1.
This is line 2.
This is line 3."""
print(text)
hint: After creating the triple-quoted string, use print(text).

title: Access first character
prompt: Given word = "python", print the first character.
hint: The first character is at index 0.

title: Access second character
prompt: Given word = "python", print the second character.
hint: The second character is at index 1.

title: Access last character
prompt: Given word = "python", print the last character using a negative index.
hint: Use -1 for the last character.

title: Loop through a string
prompt: Given word = "cat", use a loop to print each character on its own line.
hint: Use for ch in word.

title: Find string length
prompt: Given text = "banana", print its length.
hint: Use len() to count characters.

title: Slice first part
prompt: Given word = "python", print the slice from index 0 to 2.
hint: This slice should give the first two letters.

title: Slice middle part
prompt: Given word = "python", print the slice from index 2 to 5.
hint: Start at index 2 and stop before index 5.

title: Slice from the start
prompt: Given word = "python", print the first four characters using slicing.
hint: Leave the start empty.

title: Slice to the end
prompt: Given word = "python", print all characters from index 3 to the end.
hint: Leave the end empty.

title: Combine strings
prompt: Create first = "Hello" and second = "World", combine them with a space between, store the result in result, and print it.
answer:
first = "Hello"
second = "World"
result = first + " " + second
print(result)
hint: Use + and add a space string between them.

title: Repeat a string
prompt: Print the string "ha" repeated 3 times.
hint: Use * with the number 3.

title: Check text in string true
prompt: Given text = "I like Python", print whether "Python" is inside the string.
answer:
text = "I like Python"
print("Python" in text)
hint: Use the in operator.

title: Check text in string false
prompt: Given text = "I like Python", print whether "Java" is inside the string.
answer:
text = "I like Python"
print("Java" in text)
hint: Use the in operator with Java.

title: Uppercase a string
prompt: Given text = "  hello world  ", print the uppercase version.
hint: Use the upper() method.

title: Lowercase a string
prompt: Given text = "  hello world  ", print the lowercase version.
hint: Use the lower() method.

title: Strip spaces
prompt: Given text = "  hello world  ", remove the spaces at the beginning and end, then print the result.
hint: Use strip() to remove outside spaces.

title: Title case a string
prompt: Given text = "  hello world  ", print it in title case.
hint: Use title() to capitalize each word.

title: Capitalize a string
prompt: Given text = "  hello world  ", print it with only the first letter uppercase.
hint: Use capitalize().

title: Replace text
prompt: Given text = "I like cats", replace cats with dogs, store the result in new_text, and print it.
answer:
text = "I like cats"
new_text = text.replace("cats", "dogs")
print(new_text)
hint: Use replace(old, new).

title: Split a string
prompt: Given text = "red,green,blue", split it by commas into a list called colors and print the list.
hint: Use split with a comma string.

title: Join words into a sentence
prompt: Given words = ["I", "love", "Python"], join them with spaces into a variable called sentence and print it.
hint: The separator goes before .join().

title: Format with f-string
prompt: Given name = "Amy" and age = 12, print: My name is Amy and I am 12 years old. Use an f-string.
answer:
name = "Amy"
age = 12
print(f"My name is {name} and I am {age} years old.")
hint: Use f before the string and curly braces for variables.

title: Format with format method
prompt: Given name = "Amy" and age = 12, print: My name is Amy and I am 12 years old. Use format().
answer:
name = "Amy"
age = 12
print("My name is {} and I am {} years old.".format(name, age))
hint: Use {} placeholders and then .format(name, age).

title: Print escaped quotes
prompt: Print the text: He said, "Hello!"
hint: Use \" to include quote marks inside the string.

title: Print with newline
prompt: Print Line1 and Line2 on separate lines using one string.
hint: Use \n for a new line.

title: Print with tab
prompt: Print A and B with a tab space between them.
hint: Use \t for a tab.

title: Create your name string
prompt: Create a string called my_name with your name in it.
hint: Use any name as a string.

title: Print first and last character
prompt: Given name = "python", print the first and last character.
hint: Use index 0 and -1.

title: Print string length practice
prompt: Given name = "python", print its length.
hint: Use len(name).

title: Uppercase practice
prompt: Given name = "python", print it in uppercase.
hint: Use upper().

title: Replace practice
prompt: Given name = "python", replace "py" with "my" and print the result.
hint: Use replace(old, new).

title: Split sentence into words
prompt: Given text = "I love Python", split it into a list of words and print the list.
hint: split() with no argument splits by spaces.

title: Use f-string for age and subject
prompt: Create age = 12 and subject = "Math", then use an f-string to print: I am 12 years old and my favorite subject is Math.
answer:
age = 12
subject = "Math"
print(f"I am {age} years old and my favorite subject is {subject}.")
hint: Use curly braces inside an f-string.

title: Small practice example full
prompt: Write the full program that sets name = "python", prints the first character, last character, length, uppercase form, and the result of replacing "py" with "my".
answer:
name = "python"
print(name[0])
print(name[-1])
print(len(name))
print(name.upper())
print(name.replace("py", "my"))
hint: Combine all the small practice steps into one program.
